title: Doe national quantum research centers reach milestone breakthrough towards building scalable quantum computers
why:
- Researchers at Fermilab and MIT Lincoln Laboratory successfully trapped and manipulated ions using in-vacuum cryoelectronics, reducing thermal noise and improving sensitivity.
- The collaboration demonstrated a proof-of-principle experiment integrating specialized cryoelectronics with ion traps, a key step towards scalable ion-trap quantum computers.
- This hybrid approach replaces some room-temperature controls with a chip inside the cryogenic environment, paving the way for more practical large-scale quantum systems by handling ion movement and control within the cryostat.
- The project showcased the feasibility of using low-power cryoelectronics inside ion-trap systems, which could accelerate the timeline for scaling up quantum computers.
- The effort highlights the importance of multi-institutional collaboration between DOE national quantum research centers to advance scalable quantum computing technologies.
